name: Room Connection Graph
description: A graph structure that defines rooms as nodes and doorways, corridors, locks, or transitions as edges before or during level assembly.
design_notes: Room connection graphs determine whether a generated level is navigable, paced, and solvable. A useful graph records graph_node_id, graph_edge_id, critical_path_node_count, branch_probability, loop_connection_budget, lock/key ordering, and validation failures.
design_purpose: Control progression through generated rooms before detailed geometry is placed.
Guarantee reachable starts, goals, keys, locks, shops, secrets, and exits.
Give designers topology knobs such as loops, branches, dead ends, and critical path length.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are exploring a coherent dungeon or facility with routes, loops, and discoveries rather than disconnected rooms.

parameters.name: graph_node_id
parameters.description: Stable identifier for a room or logical space in the generated graph.

parameters.name: graph_edge_id
parameters.description: Identifier for a connection between two graph nodes.

parameters.name: critical_path_node_count
parameters.description: Number of nodes on the required route from start to main objective.

parameters.name: branch_probability
parameters.description: Chance or weight for adding optional branches from critical path nodes.

parameters.name: loop_connection_budget
parameters.description: Maximum extra edges that create shortcuts, alternate routes, or cycles.

edge_cases: A lock appears before its key or ability gate, causing a soft-lock.
Doorway sockets selected by room_template_id do not physically align even though the graph edge exists.
Optional branches consume all valid room templates and leave no fallback for the critical path.
Loop connections bypass required tutorial, boss, or reward nodes.
common_bugs: Connectivity validation checks graph edges but not actual spawned doorway collisions or navmesh reachability.
Random graph traversal uses unordered sets and produces different topology from the same seed.
Dead-end treasure rooms are selected for mandatory objectives because room tags are too broad.
Debug maps show room layout but not graph_edge_id reasons for rejected connections.
balancing_notes: Critical path length drives session duration more reliably than total room count.
Loops reduce backtracking frustration but can weaken lock/key or chase pacing if overused.
Optional branch rewards should match branch risk and distance from the main route.
accessibility_notes: Generated maps should represent graph structure clearly when navigation complexity is high.
Avoid procedurally creating maze-like graphs without landmarks, map tools, or pathfinding support.

implementation_notes.unity: Generate a logical graph first, validate lock/key ordering, then instantiate room_template_id prefabs by doorway sockets; draw graph_node_id and graph_edge_id overlays in editor tools.
implementation_notes.godot: Represent topology as data dictionaries or Resources before scene instancing; run reachability and soft-lock validation before adding room scenes to the tree.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use DataAssets for room templates and a generator subsystem that builds graph nodes/edges, validates critical path and loops, then spawns level instances or PCG content.
implementation_notes.web: Keep the graph as JSON-serializable data with stable node and edge IDs; export failed seeds with graph and validation_retries for reproducible bug reports.

example_games.title: The Binding of Isaac
example_games.note: Uses generated room layouts connected into floor maps.

example_games.title: Dead Cells
example_games.note: Uses generated level routes with branches and exits.
